text: William is building KORA as a local-first AI system with continuity, inspectability, and real memory as first-class features.
KORA now has a manual memory write path.
William wants continuity to be first-class.
Decision: startup_context is canonical boot continuity. facts.jsonl is durable fact memory. ledger.jsonl is raw append-only history.
Decision: Claude-KORA bridge is live. Claude can now write notes and handoffs into KORA's inspectable memory. Context pull is working.

UNRESOLVED: KORA needs to convert natural language into actionable tasks.
ACTION: Implement a natural language processing (NLP) module that can interpret user commands and translate them into specific actions.
REASONING: This action addresses the gap by enabling KORA to move beyond the current chat/council loop and start executing tasks as requested by the user, thus bringing it closer to the goal of being a voice-friendly action agent.

Pulse result: KORA identified the top unresolved issue as translating natural language into actionable tasks. This confirms the next build focus is interpreter/action routing, not more continuity scaffolding.
